summary: Dial in the parallel-development decomposition for the diagnostic-diagram rebuild: scope each unit against the real repo, hunt overlaps + gaps + parallel-safety, synthesize a clean parallel work-order set with pinned contracts.

cards.purpose: C1 is the additive type-and-loader contract that every kit part and every step shape reads. It extends the existing loadSystemTopology -> SystemTopology contract in lib/diagnostics/load-system-topology.ts so each test_action (a "step") and each part carries the fields the assembly engine (T3), templates (T4), and Figma-kit consumers (T2) need to pick a shape, place a part in a slot, draw exactly one meter hookup, and render an honest verdict. It defines the EXACT TS shapes (extended TopologyTestAction, TopologyBranch, TopologyScenario, and a new optional isOutOfRange map) plus the loader SELECT changes that surface columns that already exist but are dropped. Boundary: this unit OWNS the type shape and the loader's additive SELECT/assembly changes ONLY. It does NOT write the migration (that is T1's job), does NOT author data, does NOT touch any rendering component, and does NOT add per-case logic. Every change is additive: new optional keys, new nullable columns referenced read-only; zero consumer breaks.
consumesContracts: none — C1 is the root seam; C2 (part-API) and C3 (slot-interface) are designed against C1's types but C1 does not consume them

cards.purpose: C3 defines the seam between the assembly-engine (T3, which resolves which data-derived parts go where) and the per-step-shape layout templates (T4, which place named anchor points), and through which the kit part components (C2/T2) actually render. It is the single source of truth for (a) the closed vocabulary of slot names a step-shape may declare, (b) the StepShape enum and the deterministic observationMethod+stepKind -> StepShape selector signature, and (c) the fill protocol: a pure, no-AI function shape that takes a resolved SystemTopology + the current step (a test_action) + active scenario and emits a ResolvedScene = ordered slot-fills (component part, wire(s), exactly-one overlay, gauge/none) that a template lays out. Its boundary is types + the function/render-prop signatures ONLY — it owns no layout coordinates (T4), no data SELECT/migration (T1/C1), no part visuals (T2/C2), and no resolver business logic (T3). It is the contract everyone else compiles against.

cards.definitionOfDone: "slot-interface.ts compiles standalone and exports: (1) the closed SlotName union, (2) the StepShape union covering all 8 v1-first-class + 2 v1-plain shapes, (3) selectStepShape's type signature mapping the 9-enum observationMethod + nullable stepKind onto StepShape with NO shape unmapped and NO AI, (4) SlotFill + ResolvedScene + AssembleScene + StepTemplate + Slot prop types, (5) a typed per-shape const table declaring required/optional/forbidden slots that statically encodes spec section 4's hard rule (electrical-only terminals/overlay; pressure never electrical; locate suppresses gauge; fork = one route slot only). DoD is met when: T3 can write an AssembleScene returning ResolvedScene and T4 can write a StepTemplate consuming it WITHOUT either editing this file; the slot-rule table round-trips the spec section-5 table 1:1; the test file asserts every StepShape has an entry and forbidden slots are absent; tsc passes and the PGlite unit suite is untouched (contract is type-only, no DB, no runtime data path). No 'AI' string, no 'step N of M' concept anywhere in the surface."

It REFERENCES (does not author) the C1-proposed columns: test_actions.stepKind (proposed-new) and pin_scenario_readings.isOutOfRange (proposed-new); if C1 renames/drops either, C3's selectStepShape + verdict-slot typing adjust

spec section 5 slot tables + plan section 2 catalog are the authoritative source for the slot names and per-shape membership — these are doc assets, not data
cards.parallelSafety: "High parallel-safety as a CONTRACT-FIRST unit: it creates only NEW files in a NEW lib/diagnostics/diagram/ directory, touches no existing consumer (load-system-topology.ts, topology-flow.ts, the dot-canvas components are all left alone — those are T6's to swap). No shared mutable state, no runtime, no DB. The ONE coordination hazard is intrinsic to being a contract: its type surface is imported by T3 and T4, so it MUST land and freeze BEFORE T3/T4 fork — it is a sequencing gate, not a concurrent-edit conflict. Co-owns the new diagram/ dir namespace with T3 (assemble-scene.ts) and T4 (templates/), but file boundaries are disjoint. Mild dependency on C1/C2 type names landing first; can be drafted in parallel with C1/C2 using placeholder imports and tightened once they freeze."
risksOrUnknowns: Slot-name bikeshedding: spec section 5 uses prose names (source/device-under-test/ground/downstream-or-anchor) but per-shape extras (overlay, gauge, good-vs-bad, route, location) are implied not enumerated — the exact closed set must be ratified with T4 before freeze or templates churn. Recommend ratifying against the proto-meter shapes which already realize each.

selectStepShape coverage gap: observationMethod is a 9-enum but the spec needs 10 shapes (electrical splits into probe/continuity/voltage-drop/duty by meterMode; locate needs the proposed stepKind). The selector signature must accept BOTH observationMethod AND meterMode AND stepKind, else continuity/voltage-drop/duty/locate are unreachable — confirm C1 surfaces meterMode (currently DROPPED by the loader) and stepKind (does not exist yet).

fork's 'route' slot depends on branch_logic.routesToTestActionId (currently DROPPED by loader); if C1 does not surface it, the route slot can only carry words-only (per plan), so SlotFill must permit a degraded text-only route arm — bake the honest-thin-data arm into the type now.

Overlay cardinality: spec mandates EXACTLY ONE overlay per step; encoding 'exactly one' in a types-only contract is awkward (a single nullable slot vs a list). Decide single-overlay-slot (cleaner, recommended) and make the assembler responsible for the 1-or-0 invariant.
C2 part-API not yet pinned in repo (T2 path TBD); SlotFill's part arm uses a forward/placeholder type until C2 lands — small rework risk if C2's prop shape diverges from spec section 3.

confirm shape's 'one allowed whole-system glimpse' (spec section 5) is the single exception to 'only 3-6 parts'; the ResolvedScene type must allow a 'quiet field' bulk-fill without inviting leak on other shapes — needs a distinct optional slot, not a reuse of device-under-test.

cards.purpose: Designs the entire diagnostic-diagram visual vocabulary ONCE as a Figma component set aligned to vt tokens, then ships it into the app as a deterministic, AI-free render layer that implements the part-API contract (C2). Deliverables: (a) the eight component-kind schematic symbols (pump/sensor/actuator/valve/module/mechanical/splice/connector) + the role-special symbols (ground/relay/fuse/power-source); (b) the connection + test-overlay primitives (wire-by-electricalRole, fuel-line, mechanical-link, terminal, probe-lead, two-point/voltage-drop bracket, amp-clamp ring, pressure-gauge tee, test-point marker, scope-clip deferred); (c) the missing vt design tokens the kit and topology.css depend on (the six --role-*, --vt-recede, --vt-amber-600) defined once in app/globals.css; (d) the export pipeline (SVG -> React part components) and the C2 prop interface every part implements. Boundary: T2 owns how a part LOOKS and the component that draws it given props; it does NOT own which parts/slots a step shows (T3 assembly + T4 templates), does NOT touch the loader/DB (T1), and contains zero AI and zero per-case logic — props in, pixels out.

cards.purpose: T3 is the pure, deterministic "brain" of the new diagram: two side-effect-free functions and their types, no React, no DOM, no AI, no per-case/per-system logic. (1) The SHOW-RULE — given a step (a test_action's observationMethod + optional stepKind + scenarioRequired) it returns a StepShape and the visibility budget: which part-set may render, whether exactly one test overlay shows (and which), whether the gauge shows, and the LOCK that pins/terminals may render ONLY on an electrical metering step (observationMethod === 'electrical_measurement_at_pin', or a future waveform/scope test). (2) The SLOT-RESOLVER — given (topology data, the focus step) it deterministically assigns the step's parts into the named slots a template declares (device-under-test = focus component; source = upstream end of the active 12v/power connection; ground = the ground-role connection endpoint or ground symbol; downstream/anchor = outbound fluid-line / symptom anchor), selecting the active wire(s) and the single overlay form (one-lead / two-point / clamp / gauge-tee / scope) from electricalRole + meterMode + connectionKind. Boundary: T3 decides WHAT may show and WHICH part fills WHICH slot; it does NOT decide pixel coordinates (T4 templates own that), does NOT render parts (T2/part-components own that), and does NOT load data (T1 owns the loader/columns). It is the seam between data and visuals — it owns C3 (slot-interface) and consumes C1 (data-contract) and C2 (part-API).

cards.parallelSafety: "HIGH parallel-safety. T3 creates an entirely NEW directory lib/diagnostics/diagram/ — it touches no file any other track edits. It only READS the type definitions in load-system-topology.ts (T1's edit surface) and wire-state.ts; it imports types, not values, so even if T1's loader internals churn, T3 compiles against the agreed C1 type shape. T3 has zero shared mutable state — both functions are pure and stateless. The one coordination risk is the THREE contracts (C1/C2/C3) which must be agreed as types before parallel build: T3 OWNS C3 and authors it; T3 must align SlotAssignment output to C2 (T2's part props) and its input to C1 (T1's loader types). Mitigation: land C1/C2/C3 as type-only stubs FIRST (the decomposition's stated 'contracts land first'), then T1/T2/T3/T4 fork. T3 explicitly must NOT modify topology-flow.ts/topology-node.tsx/topology-layout.ts (old dot-canvas, T6's deletion target) — resolveSlots is the new-path replacement for toFlowElements but lives separately so no collision."
risksOrUnknowns: waveform_capture / scope shape is explicitly LATER (no waveform data in repo); but real PWM components in the scene (IMV, FRP regulator, volume control valve) carry observationMethod that may be 'electrical_measurement_at_pin' NOT 'waveform_capture' (the data-model plan §6.2 flags this must be confirmed against LIVE Supabase — scene-data.json shows PWM pins but the test_actions' observationMethod values are not in the JSON dump I read, only observableProperties/pins/connections are). T3's duty-pwm shape mapping needs the live observationMethod value confirmed.

scene-data.json (lines read 1-1076 of 2047) does NOT contain the test_actions rows themselves — I confirmed components/pins/connections but the actual observationMethod-per-test and any meterMode values live further in the file or only in live Supabase. The show-rule's per-method behavior is verified by the schema enum, but the resolver's real-scene test needs the test_action rows; risk that the fixture lacks expectedValue/meterMode (consistent with 'columns exist but empty' per the data-model plan §8.1).

C2 (part-API) is owned by T2 and not yet defined; SlotAssignment's exact field shape (e.g., how a 'ground symbol' vs a 'ground component' is expressed, how an overlay's anchor pin is referenced) depends on T2's part props — if C2 shifts, T3's output type shifts. Needs the contract frozen before deep build.

The 'source = upstream end of the active 12v/power connection' rule assumes a power/12v connection exists in the step's set; in the FRP-sensor electrical scene the active wires are signal/5v-ref/low-ref (no 12v), so 'source' for a sensor-signal probe is the 5V-ref feeder, not a 12v line — the slot-resolver's source-selection must generalize beyond literal electricalRole='12v' to 'the feeding/reference endpoint', or it will leave source empty on sensor steps. Needs a precise rule decision (likely: source = upstream endpoint of the reference/power wire by direction, role-agnostic among 12v/5v-ref).

isOutOfRange is per-(pin,scenario) and PROPOSED-NEW; the precedence ladder (numeric deferred -> isOutOfRange -> branch verdict=fail -> neutral) is owned partly by the gauge/Meter (kept as-is) — T3 must expose the verdict inputs without re-implementing the gauge's verdict logic; boundary between T3's resolver and the existing Meter verdict code needs to be drawn so they don't both decide red.

Old toFlowElements renders ALL components + ALL pins unconditionally (topology-flow.ts:59-72 maps every component, topology-node.tsx renders every pin) — confirming T3+T6 fully replace this draw path is essential; if any consumer still calls toFlowElements post-swap the leak persists. cards.purpose: Delivers the per-step-shape layout layer: one declarative template per v1 first-class shape (confirm / electrical-probe / continuity-ground / single-PID / pressure-flow / look-inspect / locate / fork) that defines NAMED SLOTS and their placement geometry (relative positions, anchoring, recede tiers, camera framing, hookup attachment region) for a fixed canvas. Each template is a pure data/geometry artifact: it says WHERE slots sit and WHAT slot names exist; it does NOT resolve which part fills a slot (that is T3/assembly-engine via C3) and does NOT draw the parts (that is T2/figma-kit parts rendered via C2). Boundary: a template in == a step-shape identifier; out == an ordered set of named slots each with placement geometry + a one-overlay attachment point, deterministic and identical for every case and every system. T4 ports the validated per-shape arrangement logic that currently lives imperatively in proto-meter.html (node tiers, source-top/DUT-center/ground-bottom electrical arrangement, fork cleared-run-then-one-next, confirm whole-spine-with-one-raised, camera bbox framing) into reusable, screenshot-checkable template modules. Each template is independently buildable and visually verifiable against a stub part + stub slot data before the real assembler or kit exists.
consumesContracts: C3 — slot-interface (PRIMARY: a template declares its slots in the vocabulary C3 defines; the assembler fills them). Templates are the concrete population of the C3 slot vocabulary.
C2 — part-API (templates allocate placement boxes/anchors sized to the prop/footprint contract every kit part implements, e.g. component-symbol footprint, terminal anchor, overlay attachment region).

C1 — data-contract (templates key off the shape selector derived from observationMethod + the proposed stepKind, and reference slot-role names — source/device-under-test/ground/downstream-anchor/next/cleared — that the assembler derives from the loaded data shape: electricalRole 12v->source, electricalRole ground->ground, focus component->device-under-test, branch_logic.routesToTestActionId->next).
inputs: C3 slot-interface frozen: the exact slot-declaration shape (how a template lists named slots; what a slot record contains — name, role-key, optional/required, capacity) and how the assembler reads template output to place a part.
C2 part-API frozen ENOUGH to know each part's placement footprint + anchor points (nominal width/height per kind, terminal/overlay anchor offsets) so geometry boxes are sized correctly.

The locked shape taxonomy + the spec §5 template-to-slot table (confirm / electrical / pressure-flow / single-PID / look-inspect / locate / fork) — the source-of-truth mapping from shape to which named slots exist.

The validated per-shape arrangements already proven in .design-shots/mockups/proto-meter.html (NODES geometry, per-shape node tiers in applyStep, settleCamera bbox framing, the electrical pins-only lock) — the reference geometry to port, NOT the build target.

The vt token surface (bone ramp, --vt-signal-*, Instrument Serif/JetBrains Mono) and the six --role-* / --vt-recede / --vt-amber-600 tokens once T2/data-work defines them in globals.css — for any spacing/recede values templates express in token terms.

A canvas/viewport convention (coordinate space + how the renderer maps template geometry to screen, desktop + 375px) agreed with T3/T5 — whether normalized 0..1 slot coords or a fixed world like the prototype's 1320x760.
outputs: One template module per v1 first-class shape (7 templates covering the 8 shapes; continuity-ground shares the electrical template, sub-typed via meterMode), each exporting: an ordered list of NAMED SLOTS (e.g. electrical = source/device-under-test/ground/downstream-anchor; pressure = upstream/device/downstream + gauge-tee point; single-PID = device + gauge; look = device + good-vs-bad; locate = part-to-find + location-context; fork = cleared-run + next-device; confirm = symptom-part-raised + quiet-field) plus placement geometry per slot (position/anchor, recede tier, the single test-overlay attachment region, and camera/framing hint).

A shape->template resolver stub/registry mapping the derived shape identifier (from observationMethod + stepKind) to its template — a thin lookup T3 plugs into (final ownership of selection may sit in T3; T4 provides the table).

Per-template snapshot/visual harness fixtures: each template rendered with placeholder slot fills (stub parts) at desktop AND 375px, producing screenshots checkable against the prototype's validated shapes.
A short README/contract note stating each template's slot names + geometry semantics so T3 (assembler) and T5 (mobile) consume them without guessing.
Unit/snapshot tests asserting each template emits the exact named slots for its shape and stable geometry (deterministic — no AI, no per-case input).
filesAndAreas: lib/diagnostics/diagram/templates/ (NEW dir) — one module per shape: confirm.ts, electrical.ts, pressure-flow.ts, single-pid.ts, look-inspect.ts, locate.ts, fork.ts (electrical.ts also serves continuity-ground + duty-PWM + voltage-drop plain variants via sub-type, not separate files).

cards.parallelSafety: SAFE to develop in parallel once C1/C2/C3 are frozen. T4 creates an entirely NEW directory (lib/diagnostics/diagram/templates/) and touches NO existing file — it does not modify the loader, the dot-canvas components, the screen, the schema, or the Figma kit, so it shares no mutable state with T1/T2/T3/T5/T6. The only shared SURFACES are the three contracts (consumed read-only) and the canvas-coordinate + 375px conventions (must be agreed up front with T3/T5, otherwise templates and the assembler/mobile layer disagree on coordinate space). Risk of silent overlap is with T3: the shape->template REGISTRY and the resolver could be claimed by both — must be explicitly assigned (recommend T4 owns the template modules + registry table; T3 owns the data->slot resolution that consumes it). T5 adapts the same template files for 375px — coordinate whether mobile variants are separate files or responsive params in the same module to avoid edit collisions.
risksOrUnknowns: C3 boundary ambiguity: whether the slot-DECLARATION (what slots a shape has) lives in the template (T4) or the slot-interface (T3/C3). Recommendation: C3 defines the slot VOCABULARY/record-type; T4 populates concrete slot sets per shape. Must be settled before either starts or both will define overlapping types.

Coordinate-space convention is unspecified: the prototype uses a fixed 1320x760 world with absolute node coords + a camera that fits a bbox per shape; the new system may want normalized slot coords. Templates' geometry semantics hinge on this — needs a one-line decision with T3/T5.

Camera/framing-per-shape (settleCamera in the prototype, including the confirm whole-spine 0.82 scale and the 0.42 floor) is layout-adjacent: is framing part of the template (T4) or the renderer (T6)? Recommend the template carries a framing HINT; the renderer applies it.
375px is a hard gate but mobile variant ownership straddles T4/T5 — confirm split (responsive params vs separate mobile templates) to avoid collisions.

stepKind is a proposed-new nullable column (not yet in schema) and drives the locate template + disambiguates confirm; if T1 does not land it, the locate template has no clean trigger and must fall back to a derived heuristic — template must be buildable against a stubbed shape selector regardless.

Fork template needs branch_logic.routesToTestActionId (exists in schema, currently dropped by loader) surfaced by C1 to know the 'next' slot's part; until C1 lands, fork template is built+screenshotted against stub 'next'/'cleared' fills.

Slot count vs scene reality: the electrical template's downstream/anchor slot and the confirm template's 'quiet field' must gracefully handle the 25-part scene without leaking the whole map — verify the quiet-field is a single backdrop slot, not 24 placed parts, so it does not reintroduce the spaghetti the spec rejects.

cards.purpose: This unit is the seam-closer and the gate. It owns no contract and writes no kit part, assembler rule, or template. It connects the six tracks into one running chain on the ONE real route (app/curator/topology/page.tsx → loader → assembly-engine → templates → kit parts), confirms the dot-canvas draw path is gone from that route, then proves the result against the real 2018 F-250 6.7L P0087 fuel scene at desktop AND 375px. It produces the evidence that the leak-class bug is dead (a pressure step shows no 12V/GND pin), that provenance is honest (graphite drafted / amber needs-field-check from labelGap·electricalContract, never red unless gated), that there is zero AI in the draw path, and that there is zero per-case / per-system layout code. Its boundary: it adapts/wires and validates; it does not author parts, rules, templates, mobile variants, the migration, or the loader SELECT — those are T1–T6. If a track is incomplete, this unit's job is to surface the exact gap, not to fill it.

lenses.summary: The decomposition's biggest risk is that each of the three contracts (C1/C2/C3) is authored TWICE — once as a standalone "contract" card and once inside a track card that claims `ownsContract`. C1 is claimed by both the "C1 Data contract" card and T1; C2 by both "C2 Part component API" and T2; C3 by both "C3 Slot/template interface" and T3. In every case the two cards name DIFFERENT files and DIFFERENT directory roots for the same types, so if both run they will produce duplicate, diverging type modules and merge-conflict on schema.ts / load-system-topology.ts. This must be collapsed: one owner per contract, the track absorbs the contract card (or the contract card is the deliverable and the track consumes it) — not both. Second-order: the kit lives at three different paths across cards (components/diagnostic-diagram/parts vs components/diagram-kit vs components/diagram), and StepShape/SlotName/ResolvedScene are defined in three places (C3's slot-interface.ts, T3's slot-resolver.ts/show-rule.ts, T4's templates/types.ts). Third, the loader-edit surface (load-system-topology.ts type exports + SELECTs + schema.ts table blocks) is claimed by BOTH C1 and T1 as a write target, and INTEGRATION+T6 both edit topology-diagnostic.tsx. Several overlaps are amplified by factual errors in the cards: electricalRole/fromPinId/toPinId are ALREADY surfaced by the loader (lines 241-243), and branch_logic.verdict (ok|warn|fail|impossible) is already surfaced — so T3's verdict-precedence and the "un-drop wires" work partly does not exist and risks two units re-touching already-correct code. Resolution throughout: merge each contract into a single owning unit, reserve one path per artifact, and pin the actually-dropped vs already-present fields before forking.

findings.where: C1 'Data contract' card AND T1 'Data deltas' card — both ownsContract C1; both edit lib/diagnostics/load-system-topology.ts (type exports + SELECTs) and lib/db/schema.ts (testActions + pinScenarioReadings blocks) and tests/unit/promote-system-data.test.ts
findings.detail: The decomposition has C1 as a standalone contract card AND T1 as a track that 'ownsContract: C1', and they list the SAME mutable files: load-system-topology.ts type exports (TopologyTestAction/TopologyBranch/TopologyScenario) + the testActions/branchLogic/readingRows SELECTs, lib/db/schema.ts (add stepKind to testActions ~line 886, add isOutOfRange to pinScenarioReadings lines 1099-1113), and the loadSystemTopology round-trip test. C1's card even says 'C1 owns the schema.ts TS-side col defs and T1 owns the .sql DDL' — but T1's own card lists 'schema.ts deltas: testActions.stepKind ... pinScenarioReadings.isOutOfRange' as ITS output. Two units editing the same three files = guaranteed merge conflict and duplicated/diverging type definitions. There is no real seam between them; C1 is not a separable contract from T1, it IS T1's type+loader work.
findings.resolution: Collapse C1 into T1. Make T1 the single owner of the entire C1 surface: schema.ts col defs, the migration DDL, the loader SELECT widening, AND the TopologyTestAction/Branch/Scenario type exports + the verdict-precedence doc note. Delete the standalone 'C1 Data contract' card as a separate buildable unit; keep its CONTENT as the spec/DoD that T1 must satisfy and that every other track reviews-and-freezes before forking. No file is touched by two units.
findings.owner: T1 (data) — absorbs C1 entirely

findings.where: C2 'Part component API' card (components/diagnostic-diagram/parts/part-contract.ts) AND T2 'Figma kit' card (components/diagram-kit/part-api.ts) — both ownsContract C2, both define the part prop interface + a kind→component registry, at DIFFERENT paths
findings.detail: C2 says it owns DiagramPartProps in components/diagnostic-diagram/parts/part-contract.ts + part-registry.ts + stubs/. T2 says it owns the C2 part-API in components/diagram-kit/part-api.ts + registry.ts + the real exported part components. Both claim ownsContract C2. So the prop interface, the registry, and the part directory are each authored twice under two different directory roots. If both run, T3/T4/T5/T6 import from one path while the other unit ships the other path → duplicate contracts, ambiguous imports, and two registries that drift. C2's stubs and T2's real components also overlap (both produce a component per kind).
findings.resolution: One owner, one path. Recommend: T2 owns the C2 contract file + the real part components + the registry (it is the unit that knows the visual footprint and must implement the interface anyway). Reserve a SINGLE directory root — pick components/diagram-kit/ — for all parts, the part-api.ts contract, and registry.ts. Fold C2's value (typed stubs so T3/T4 can render before Figma art lands) into T2 as a 'land the interface + placeholder stubs FIRST, real art second' sequencing inside T2. Delete the standalone C2 card as a separate unit. Every consumer imports components/diagram-kit/part-api.ts only.
findings.owner: T2 (figma-kit) — absorbs C2; single path components/diagram-kit/

findings.where: C3 'Slot/template interface' card (lib/diagnostics/diagram/slot-interface.ts) AND T3 'Assembly engine' card (lib/diagnostics/diagram/slot-resolver.ts + show-rule.ts) — both ownsContract C3, both define SlotName / StepShape / ResolvedScene / SlotFill / selectStepShape
findings.detail: C3 card output: slot-interface.ts exporting SlotName, StepShape, SlotFill, ResolvedScene, AssembleScene, StepTemplate, selectStepShape signature, and a per-shape required/optional/forbidden slot table. T3 card: 'slot-resolver.ts ... OWNS C3 slot-interface types: SlotName, SlotAssignment, ResolvedScene' and show-rule.ts exporting 'a StepShape union'. Both claim ownsContract C3 and both define the SAME core types (StepShape, SlotName, ResolvedScene). This is the seam that T4/T5/T6 all compile against, so a double-definition here ripples into four units. T3's card even renames SlotFill→SlotAssignment, signalling the two authors have already diverged on the shape.
findings.resolution: Split cleanly: C3 = the pure type/signature module ONLY (SlotName, StepShape, SlotFill, ResolvedScene, AssembleScene, StepTemplate, selectStepShape SIGNATURE, the forbidden-slot const table) at lib/diagnostics/diagram/slot-interface.ts, and it LANDS FIRST as a frozen contract. T3 = the IMPLEMENTATIONS ONLY (the body of resolveStepShape + computeShowBudget + resolveSlots) importing those types, never re-declaring them. Assign C3 to a single owner (recommend T3's owner authors slot-interface.ts as deliverable #1, then implements against it) so one person controls the shape. Remove the duplicate type declarations from T3's slot-resolver.ts/show-rule.ts — they import from slot-interface.ts.
findings.owner: T3 (assembly-engine) — authors slot-interface.ts (C3) as its first deliverable, then implements against it; types live ONLY in slot-interface.ts

findings.where: T4 templates/types.ts redefines StepShape + SlotName/SlotRole + Template types that C3/T3 already own in slot-interface.ts
findings.detail: T4's filesAndAreas lists 'lib/diagnostics/diagram/templates/types.ts (NEW) — the StepShape union, SlotName/SlotRole types, Template + SlotPlacement types' with a parenthetical 'these likely re-export or import the C3 slot-interface types T3 owns; T4 must NOT redefine C3.' The note acknowledges the hazard but the file still lists StepShape + SlotName as defined there. Two modules exporting StepShape/SlotName = the classic 'which import wins' bug; templates and the resolver can silently disagree on the shape vocabulary, which is exactly the kind of leak the whole feature is trying to prevent.
findings.resolution: T4 imports StepShape, SlotName, ResolvedScene, StepTemplate, Slot from slot-interface.ts and defines ONLY genuinely template-local types (SlotPlacement geometry, camera-framing hint, the per-shape concrete slot SETS). The shape↔template registry (registry.ts) is fine in T4 as long as it maps the C3-owned StepShape to a template — it must not introduce a parallel StepShape. Add to the contract-freeze gate: 'no track re-declares SlotName/StepShape/ResolvedScene; grep proves a single declaration site.'
findings.owner: T4 (templates) — consumes C3 types, owns only SlotPlacement/geometry + registry

findings.where: Kit directory root contradiction: components/diagnostic-diagram/parts/ (C2) vs components/diagram-kit/ (T2) vs components/diagram/ (T5: diagram-mobile.css, meter-sheet.tsx, step-template.tsx)
findings.detail: Three different top-level directories are reserved for the same diagram surface across cards. C2 reserves components/diagnostic-diagram/parts/. T2 reserves components/diagram-kit/. T5 puts diagram-mobile.css, meter-sheet.tsx and a step-template.tsx under components/diagram/. T5 also claims components/diagram/step-template.tsx is 'T4-OWNED' — but T4's card puts its templates under lib/diagnostics/diagram/templates/ (a lib path, not a components path). So the template component's home is itself ambiguous (lib vs components), and the kit has three candidate roots. Parallel agents will scatter files across all three and imports will be a guessing game; verified the repo has NONE of these dirs today, so nothing anchors the choice.
findings.resolution: Pin the directory layout in the contract phase, before any track forks: (a) parts/contract/registry → components/diagram-kit/ (single root, owned by T2); (b) pure logic (slot-interface, show-rule, slot-resolver) → lib/diagnostics/diagram/ (owned by T3); (c) templates → decide ONE home — recommend the React template components live under components/diagram-kit/templates/ (T4) since they render parts, while their geometry data can stay pure; (d) mobile css/sheet → components/diagram-kit/ alongside templates (T5). Write the agreed tree into the kickoff so each agent reserves only its subtree.
findings.owner: new unit: contract-phase 'directory + path freeze' (owned by whoever lands contracts first; enforce in T1/T2/T3 kickoff)

findings.where: T4 'shape→template registry' AND T3 'shape selection' — both touch the StepShape→template/handler mapping; T4 registry.ts vs T3 resolveStepShape
findings.detail: T4 outputs 'a shape→template resolver stub/registry mapping the derived shape identifier (from observationMethod + stepKind) to its template — a thin lookup T3 plugs into (final ownership of selection may sit in T3).' T3 outputs resolveStepShape(step)→StepShape (the observationMethod→shape map). The card text explicitly flags 'final ownership of selection may sit in T3' — i.e. it is UNRESOLVED who owns shape selection vs shape→template lookup. If both build it, you get two selectors that can disagree (T3 says shape=pressure-flow, T4's registry maps a different derivation).
findings.resolution: Draw the line precisely: T3 owns selectStepShape (data → StepShape, the single source of which shape). T4 owns the shape → template-module lookup table (StepShape → React template), which is a pure map keyed on the T3-produced StepShape and contains NO re-derivation from observationMethod/stepKind. Document in C3: 'observationMethod/stepKind are read ONLY by selectStepShape; templates/registry key only on the resulting StepShape.'
findings.owner: T3 owns selectStepShape; T4 owns StepShape→template registry (no re-derivation)

findings.where: INTEGRATION card AND T6 card both edit components/screens/topology-diagnostic.tsx and app/curator/topology/page.tsx
findings.detail: T6's outputs: 'components/screens/topology-diagnostic.tsx rewired ... app/curator/topology/page.tsx ... layoutTopology call REMOVED.' INTEGRATION's filesAndAreas: 'components/screens/topology-diagnostic.tsx — the host screen; T6 swaps ... integration wires the props ... and confirms the swap end-to-end' and 'app/curator/topology/page.tsx — ... integration verifies the full chain ... and decides whether layoutTopology() is still called.' Both name these two files as edit targets, and INTEGRATION says it 'decides whether layoutTopology() is still called' — that is a code edit to the same page T6 owns. INTEGRATION's own parallelSafety admits 'It shares mutable state with T6 ... cannot run concurrently with T6.'
findings.resolution: Make the edit ownership exclusive: T6 is the ONLY unit that writes topology-diagnostic.tsx and app/curator/topology/page.tsx (including the layoutTopology removal/keep decision). INTEGRATION is READ-ONLY on those two files plus its own NEW files (.design-shots/scene-walk.mjs, tests/unit/diagnostic-scene-assembly.test.ts). findings.where: FACTUAL ERROR amplifying overlap: multiple cards claim electricalRole/fromPinId/toPinId are 'DROPPED by the loader' — they are ALREADY surfaced (load-system-topology.ts lines 241-243, 519). branch_logic.verdict is also already surfaced (line 322).

If T1 and T3 both act on the false 'wires are dropped' premise, they may both touch the connection SELECT/type that is already correct, or build degradation paths for data that is present — wasted, conflicting edits on the same lines.
findings.resolution: Correct the contract before forking. T1's actual loader work is narrow: add meterMode/expectedValue/expectedUnit/expectedTolerance to the testActions SELECT (line ~303) and routesToTestActionId/reasoning to the branchLogic SELECT (line ~322), plus surface symptom_test_implications.priority (currently the loader only computes membership via implRows at 334-345, it drops priority — and note priority is .notNull() in schema line 1142, so no null-handling needed). findings.where: test_actions.stepKind does NOT exist (verified schema lines 861-911 have no stepKind) — it is genuinely new and gates T3's 'locate' shape, T4's locate template, and C3's selectStepShape signature
findings.detail: Confirmed: the testActions table (schema.ts 861-911) has observationMethod (9-enum), meterMode, expectedValue/Unit/Tolerance, expectedObservation, invasiveness, etc. — but NO stepKind/step_kind column. observationMethod's 9 enum values are scan_tool_pid, pressure_test_with_gauge, electrical_measurement_at_pin, waveform_capture, direct_visual_internal, direct_visual_external, audible, touch, smell. The 'locate' shape and the probe/continuity/voltage-drop/duty split cannot be derived from observationMethod alone (electrical_measurement_at_pin must split into probe/continuity/voltage-drop via meterMode; locate needs stepKind). So selectStepShape MUST consume observationMethod AND meterMode AND stepKind. Three units (T1 migration, T3 selector, T4 locate template) depend on stepKind existing; if T1 slips, all three stall or build heuristic fallbacks that then conflict.
findings.resolution: Sequence stepKind as a hard contract-phase deliverable in T1 (migration 0024 ADD COLUMN step_kind text + journal idx 26 + schema.ts def + apply-to-staging), with its vocabulary (small documented string union) pinned in the same review. selectStepShape's signature in slot-interface.ts must take (observationMethod, meterMode, stepKind) — confirm meterMode is surfaced by T1 in the same pass since continuity/voltage-drop/duty are unreachable without it. T3/T4 build against the typed-nullable stepKind so they compile pre-migration, but the migration must land before integration.
findings.owner: T1 (data) owns the stepKind column + meterMode surfacing; T3 owns the 3-arg selectStepShape signature

findings.where: Meter/reading verdict logic: T3 'verdict precedence' vs the existing/kept Meter block (T2/T4) vs T5's meter-sheet.tsx — who decides 'red'
findings.detail: T3 outputs verdict inputs (isOutOfRange + branch verdict) and its card warns 'the boundary between T3's resolver and the existing Meter verdict code needs to be drawn so they don't both decide red.' T5 builds meter-sheet.tsx wrapping 'the T2/T4 Meter/reading block.' The Meter is described as 'kept as-is' (spec §7) but it currently lives in the topology surface being replaced, and three units (T3 computes verdict gating, the Meter renders verdict color, T5 wraps it in a sheet) all touch the verdict→color path. Two units deciding 'red' is exactly the provenance-honesty risk the feature must avoid.
findings.resolution: Single decision point: T3's resolver emits a typed verdict signal (out-of-range | branch-fail | neutral) as pure data; the Meter component RENDERS that signal and contains zero independent red-deciding logic (no prose-number parsing). T5's meter-sheet.tsx is layout-only (detents/safe-area) and passes the verdict signal through untouched. Pin in C3: 'verdict is computed once in the resolver; the Meter is a pure renderer of the verdict signal.' Add an INTEGRATION assertion: nothing colors red unless isOutOfRange=true OR branch verdict=fail.
findings.owner: T3 computes the verdict signal; the Meter (T2/T4) renders it; T5 passes through

findings.where: topology-selection-context.tsx + topology-detail-panel.tsx selection model — T6 'DELETE or fold into assembler' vs the kept detail-panel/selection surface vs C3's per-step resolved set
findings.detail: T6 lists topology-selection-context.tsx as 'DELETE or fold into assembler' and notes topology-detail-panel.tsx 'is currently driven by component/pin/connection selection from the xyflow node tree; in the assembler world selection may no longer be node-clicks — need to confirm whether the detail panel + selection model survive the swap.' The screen (verified topology-diagnostic.tsx) wires SelectionState + diagramSelection + panelSelection tightly to the xyflow node tree. detail-panel imports TopologyComponent/Connection/Pin/Scenario types. So 'kept surface' (detail-panel) and 'deleted surface' (selection context, node tree) are entangled, and it's unspecified whether the new assembler reproduces click-selection. This is a contract gap between C3 (resolved element set) and T6 (what's clickable).
findings.resolution: Decide in C3/T6 contract review whether the new assembler exposes a selection/click model at all. Simplest path consistent with the spec: the per-step assembler shows only the current step's parts + the Meter, so the free-form detail-panel selection may be unnecessary — fold any needed per-part info into the step surface and retire topology-selection-context.tsx + the panel with the xyflow canvas. If selection IS kept, C3 must expose the resolved element set so the panel can map a click to a part. Make T6 own this decision and document it before deleting.
findings.owner: T6 (app-swap) — owns the selection-model decision; coordinate the resolved-element-set exposure with T3/C3

findings.where: INTEGRATION's tests/unit/diagnostic-scene-assembly.test.ts leak assertion depends on C3 exposing a per-step 'resolved element set' that no card guarantees as a public output
findings.detail: INTEGRATION's risk note: 'The leak invariant needs a machine-checkable definition of electrical terminal present in the rendered set ... if C3 does not expose the resolved part set per step, integration can only validate visually.' T3's ResolvedScene/computeShowBudget produce pinsAllowed + the slot fills, which IS the element set — but C3's card describes ResolvedScene as slot-fills consumed by a template, not explicitly as a queryable element list for tests. This is a small contract gap: the deterministic leak test (the feature's core guarantee) has no guaranteed data hook.
findings.resolution: Add to C3/T3's contract: ResolvedScene exposes pinsAllowed and the full resolved element/slot set as inspectable data (it already must, for the template to consume it) so INTEGRATION's test can assert 'pressure-flow step → zero terminal/pin elements in ResolvedScene' deterministically rather than by screenshot. T3 ships a unit test proving the lock at the resolver level (its DoD already implies this); INTEGRATION's test consumes the same ResolvedScene.
findings.owner: T3 (assembly-engine) — guarantee ResolvedScene is inspectable; INTEGRATION consumes it

findings.where: T4/T5 ownership of the responsive template seam: T5 must add @media/responsive hooks to T4-owned components/diagram-kit template files (step-template.tsx + template CSS)
findings.detail: T5's filesAndAreas: 'components/diagram/step-template.tsx (T4-OWNED — T5 only ADDS responsive className hooks / a useViewport-or-CSS branch; must coordinate edits, this is the primary overlap surface with T4)' and 'components/diagram/*.css template styles (T4-OWNED — T5 appends mobile @media blocks).' Two units editing the same TSX + CSS files is a direct collision; T5's own card calls it 'the sharpest coordination risk.' T4's card also flags it: 'if T4 builds desktop-only with hardcoded px and no responsive seam, T5 cannot layer mobile cleanly.'
findings.resolution: Define a responsive contract in the C3/T4 freeze: each T4 template exposes a STABLE root class / data-attribute per StepShape (e.g. data-shape="pressure-flow") and T5 writes ALL responsive rules in its own diagram-mobile.css targeting those classes — T5 never edits T4's TSX. If a JS viewport branch is unavoidable, T4 exposes it as a prop T5 sets, so the TSX stays T4-owned. This makes the files single-owner (T4 TSX/desktop-CSS, T5 mobile-CSS) with a class-name seam instead of a shared-edit seam.
findings.owner: T4 owns template TSX + desktop CSS (exposes stable root classes); T5 owns diagram-mobile.css only

findings.where: drizzle/migrations/meta/_journal.json append (idx 26) is an append-only single-file write — any other concurrent migration-authoring unit collides
findings.detail: Verified journal last idx = 25 (0023_interactive_electrical_topology). findings.detail: Verified the ONLY importers of @xyflow/react/@dagrejs/dagre/topology-layout/toFlowElements/layoutTopology are: app/curator/topology/page.tsx, components/screens/topology-diagnostic.tsx, and the 5 delete-target files (topology-diagram.tsx, topology-flow.ts, topology-node.tsx, wire-edge.tsx, topology-layout.ts).
