lenses.lens: GAPS — work no unit owns, under-specified contracts, unhandled shapes/cases/states, missing validation
lenses.summary: The decomposition is coherent at the contract/track seams but has one structural hole that sinks the whole feature and several smaller gaps. BLOCKER 1: nobody owns the STEP STATE MACHINE — deriving the current step from symptom_test_implications.priority, advancing/back, and re-routing on a fork verdict. The proto runs entirely on a STEPS[] array with stepIdx/goNext/applyStep; the real screen (topology-diagnostic.tsx) has ZERO step concept — it is a whole-system browser (scenario + click-to-inspect). T3 explicitly receives "the already-selected current step" and T6 only "computes step silently" in one sentence with no owner. This is a whole missing unit. BLOCKER 2: the slot-resolver (T3) derives source/ground/wires/circuit-set from electricalRole + fromPinId + connectionKind, but on the real 6.7L scene 35/43 connections have null electricalRole and 41/43 null fromPinId, AND there is NO data edge from a test_action (single componentId FK) to its 3-6-part "circuit set" — the proto hard-codes circuit:[...]/anchor:[...]/activeWire:[...] per step, with no column to derive them from. T3 has no deterministic input for the very thing it must produce. Plus gaps: the secondary/"Operational Theori"/why-source content panel (proto's richest surface) is owned by no unit; the detail-panel click-selection model is deleted by T6 with no replacement owner; C1 wrongly calls priority "currently not surfaced/a new join" (it's .notNull() and already used by cached-lookup.ts); reduced-motion is only in T5's DoD though wire-flow animation is cross-track; and INTEGRATION's leak-test needs C3 to expose a per-step resolved element set that C3 doesn't currently promise.

findings.where: NO unit — the step state machine; touches T3 inputs, T6 (components/screens/topology-diagnostic.tsx), C3
findings.detail: The entire spec/proto premise is a step WALK: proto-meter.html has `const STEPS=[...]` (line 948), `let stepIdx=0` (1102), `applyStep()` (1170), `goNext/goBack` (1600-1601) and nav buttons — a real state machine that orders steps, advances, goes back, and on a fork re-routes to routesToTestActionId. The plan §confirms 'a step IS a test_action; order = symptom_test_implications.priority; the fork graph reroutes on a resolved verdict.' But the REAL screen components/screens/topology-diagnostic.tsx owns only scenario state + component/pin/connection click-selection — it has ZERO step concept (grep for step/implicat/priority/currentStep returns nothing). T3 says it 'receives the already-selected current step' (input #3) and T6 says 'the per-step current-test derivation ... computed silently' in one line of outputs with no DoD and no module. So the unit that (a) builds the ordered step list from priority, (b) tracks current step + advance/back, (c) routes on fork verdict, and (d) feeds T3 one step at a time is owned by NOBODY. Without it there is nothing to drive the assembler. This is the spine of the feature.
findings.resolution: Add a new track T7 (step-engine): a pure module lib/diagnostics/diagram/step-sequence.ts owning (1) buildStepSequence(topology) -> ordered TestAction[] keyed on implicatedByCurrentSymptom + symptom_test_implications.priority, (2) a currentStep + advance/back reducer, (3) resolveFork(step, verdict) -> nextStepId via branch_logic.routesToTestActionId (degrade to words-only when null). It consumes C1, sits upstream of T3 (feeds resolveSlots one step), and T6 mounts its reducer in the screen. Pure/no-AI like T3. C3's AssembleScene signature must take the engine's current step as its `step` arg (already shaped that way) — so C3 is compatible, it just lacks the producer.
findings.owner: new unit: T7 step-engine (pure step sequencing + fork routing + current-step reducer)

findings.where: T3 slot-resolver (lib/diagnostics/diagram/slot-resolver.ts) inputs; C1 contract; the real scene .design-shots/scene-data.json
findings.detail: T3's resolveSlots derives device-under-test=focus component, source=upstream end of the 12v/power connection, ground=ground-role endpoint, wires=by electricalRole, and the per-step circuit-set. VERIFIED on the real scene: 35 of 43 connections have null electricalRole and 41 of 43 have null fromPinId. So source/ground/wire selection collapses to empty on nearly every step on the exact scene INTEGRATION must validate. WORSE: there is NO data edge from a test_action to its 3-6-part circuit set — test_actions has a single componentId FK (its focus part only); the proto HARD-CODES circuit:['tank','relay','pump','filter','chassis'] / anchor:['frp'] / activeWire:['relay','pump'] per step. Nothing in the schema or C1 contract lets T3 deterministically compute which 3-6 parts belong to a step. T3's own risk list flags 'source for a sensor probe is the 5V-ref feeder not 12v' but misses the larger hole: the circuit-set itself is underivable from current data. resolveSlots has no honest input to produce its core output.
findings.resolution: Two parts. (a) C1/T1 must add the missing edge: either a step->component-set join table (test_action_circuit_components) OR confirm the circuit-set is derived by graph-walk from the focus component over connections within N hops — and if the latter, T3 must own that graph-walk and INTEGRATION must accept that on today's null-role data it yields a degraded set. (b) Make T3's DoD explicitly cover the honest-degrade path: when electricalRole/fromPinId are null (today's reality), slots fill by connectionKind + graph adjacency, source/ground may be absent, and the resolver MUST NOT fabricate. Add a slot-resolver test asserting graceful empty-source behavior on null-role connections (the real scene), not just the idealized FRP-signal case. INTEGRATION's 'render the real scene' expectation must be reset: with null roles, electrical-probe cannot show a lit source->part->ground path until a curator authors roles/pins — make that an explicit, accepted degraded outcome, not a silent failure.
findings.owner: C1/T1 (add the step->circuit-set edge or ratify graph-walk derivation) + T3 (own the derivation + honest-degrade DoD + a null-role test)

findings.where: NO unit — the secondary content / 'why? · see source' / Operational-Theori panel; proto secondary[] payload; spec §7 'kept' list omits it
findings.detail: Every proto STEP carries a rich `secondary:[...]` block (the 'why P0087 · the spine · Operational Theori' section, the 'Needs field check' amber rows, 'Operational Theori' rows) plus `why` (see-source) and `probe` (the instruction text). The plan calls this 'the provenance "from theory · why?" pull' and lists it under 'renders today.' The data sources are real (component.body/role/wireSummary/probingTactic/unknownNote already in C1's TopologyComponent; pin.missingLogic/labelGap; branch reasoning). But NO unit owns assembling and rendering this content panel. T2 owns part VISUALS, T4 owns layout slots, T5 owns the mobile sheet CHROME, T3 owns slot resolution — none owns the per-step prose/why/source/theori content block that is the trust surface (the whole 'AI as evidence with see-source' product principle). It falls through the cracks between T4 (a slot?) and T5 (the sheet body?).
findings.resolution: Assign the content panel explicitly. Cleanest: it is a named slot in C3 (e.g. 'detail'/'why-source') whose SlotFill is a content payload assembled by T3's resolver from the step's component/pin/branch prose fields, laid out by T4 inside the Meter/sheet region, and rendered by a kept-or-new content component. Add to C3 the SlotFill arm for prose content; add to T3 the assembly of {probe, why, secondary[], theori} from C1 fields; add to T4/T5 the placement. Pin the owner before fork — otherwise the single most trust-bearing surface ships unowned.
findings.owner: C3 (add the content SlotFill type) + T3 (assemble the prose payload from C1 fields) + T4/T5 (place + render it)

findings.where: T6 deletes the click-selection model (topology-detail-panel.tsx, topology-selection-context.tsx) with no replacement owner; C3 selection contract
findings.detail: The current screen's primary interaction is click-a-part/pin/connection -> TopologyDetailPanel shows its body (verified: topology-diagnostic.tsx holds SelectionState + panelSelection; topology-detail-panel.tsx renders component/pin/connection bodies with onSelectComponent/onSelectPin; topology-selection-context.tsx bridges clicks out of the xyflow nodes). T6's plan says 'topology-selection-context.tsx — DELETE or fold into assembler' and flags in its own risks 'in the assembler world selection may no longer be node-clicks — need to confirm whether the detail panel + selection model survive ... possible scope creep / contract gap with C3.' That confirmation is never resolved and no unit owns the new selection/tap model. If a tech can no longer tap a part to see its detail, that is a silent feature regression hidden inside a 'swap.'
findings.resolution: Make a decision and assign it now, not at integration. Either (a) C3 explicitly declares NO free-selection in v1 (the step IS the selection; detail comes from the current step's content panel) and T6 deletes the selection model cleanly — document this as an intentional interaction change for Brandon; OR (b) C3 adds a selection contract (which slot is tappable, what it reveals) and T3/T4 own resolving tap targets. Default to (a) per the spec's 'the screen IS the circuit you're testing' framing. Either way it must be a ratified contract line in C3, not a TODO in T6's risk list.
findings.owner: C3 (ratify the v1 selection/tap contract — recommend 'no free selection, step drives detail') + T6 (delete selection model per that decision)

findings.where: INTEGRATION leak-test depends on a per-step resolved element set that C3 does not promise to expose
findings.detail: INTEGRATION's hero deliverable is a DETERMINISTIC leak test (tests/unit/diagnostic-scene-assembly.test.ts) asserting 'a pressure step => zero electrical terminals in the rendered set.' INTEGRATION's own risk list admits: 'if C3 does not expose the resolved part set per step, integration can only validate visually ... it should require C3 to expose the per-step resolved element set.' But C3's outputs (ResolvedScene = slot->SlotFill map) and T3's ResolvedScene do not guarantee a flat, machine-enumerable 'every element that will render this step' list distinct from the slot map — and the LOCK that kills the leak (pinsAllowed=false) lives in T3's computeShowBudget, which INTEGRATION cannot import as a contract guarantee. The single most important regression guard in the whole project has no contractual data to assert against.
findings.resolution: Add to C3/T3 an explicit, exported renderedElementSet(scene) or assert ResolvedScene carries a complete enumerable element list (parts + terminals + wires + overlay) such that INTEGRATION can assert `elements.filter(e=>e.kind==='terminal').length===0` on a pressure step purely from the resolver output. Make this a named field in C3's ResolvedScene and a DoD line in T3 ('the resolved scene is fully enumerable for the leak invariant'). Then INTEGRATION's deterministic test is real, not screenshot-eyeballed.
findings.owner: C3 (add enumerable element-set to ResolvedScene) + T3 (populate it) — consumed by INTEGRATION

findings.where: C1 — priority is mis-scoped as 'not surfaced / a new join'; symptom_test_implications.priority + cached-lookup.ts
findings.detail: C1's outputs/risks say priority is 'currently surfaced only in cached-lookup.ts, not load-system-topology.ts; adding it ... means a new join' and treats it as additive-risky. VERIFIED: symptom_test_implications.priority is `priority: integer('priority').notNull()` (schema line ~1142), already indexed (symptom_test_implications_symptom_priority_idx), and already SELECTed + orderBy(asc(priority)) in cached-lookup.ts (lines 164,183,223). The loader already queries symptom_test_implications (implRows) — surfacing priority is widening an EXISTING SELECT by one notNull column, not a new join. The 'verify null-handling for non-implicated actions' risk is moot: priority is NOT NULL and only exists on implicated rows, so non-implicated actions simply have no STI row (priority absent by membership, exactly as today's implicatedByCurrentSymptom Set works).
findings.resolution: Correct C1: priority is NOT NULL, already indexed, already proven in cached-lookup.ts; surface it by adding priority to the existing implRows SELECT (change the .select to include priority and build a Map<testActionId,priority> instead of a Set). No new join, no nullable-default concern. This also de-risks T7/T3 ordering. Drop the 'new join' framing from the contract diff.
findings.owner: C1 (correct the priority framing + widen existing implRows SELECT) — T1 mirrors

findings.where: reduced-motion / wire-flow animation — only in T5 DoD, but animation is desktop+mobile and cross-track (T2 wires, T6 draw path)
findings.detail: prefers-reduced-motion appears ONLY in T5's DoD ('respected for sheet transitions and any wire flow'). But the proto animates wire flow on BOTH desktop and mobile (proto-meter.html line 843 reduceMotion guard is global, and topology.css:316 already has a desktop reduced-motion block). The animated wire-flow is owned by T2 (the wire part visuals) and rendered on the desktop draw path (T6/T4), not just mobile. If only T5 honors reduced-motion, the desktop assembled scene can animate wires for a reduced-motion user — an accessibility regression on the primary (desktop) surface that no desktop unit's DoD catches.
findings.resolution: Move reduced-motion from a T5-only concern to a C2 contract requirement: any animated part (wire flow, light pulse) MUST gate its animation on prefers-reduced-motion as a property of the part itself (CSS or prop), so it holds on desktop and mobile uniformly. Add a reduced-motion line to T2's DoD and INTEGRATION's validation gate (capture a reduced-motion frame). T5 then inherits it rather than owning it alone.
findings.owner: T2 (gate animated parts on reduced-motion in C2) + INTEGRATION (validate a reduced-motion frame) — T5 inherits

findings.where: Empty/error/no-coverage states for the assembled screen; T6 + INTEGRATION
findings.detail: loadSystemTopology returns null when platform/symptom/system/components are absent, and a covered (vehicle,DTC) pair may have implicated test_actions = 0 (no step sequence). The old screen rendered the whole topology regardless of steps; the new step-driven assembler has a genuine empty case: 'topology exists but no implicated steps' and 'loader returned null.' No unit owns the empty/zero-step/loader-null render state for the new chain. T6 outputs a working render but no DoD line for 'symptom with no steps' or 'null topology'; INTEGRATION validates only the populated P0087 scene. This is exactly the class of 'shipped broken because validated on the happy path' the standing memories warn about.
findings.resolution: Add to T6 DoD: explicit render for (a) loadSystemTopology null (existing not-found path preserved) and (b) topology present but zero implicated steps (honest 'no test plan captured for this code yet' state, no crash, no empty canvas). Add an INTEGRATION check on at least one of the other two seeded symptoms (p0088/no-start) to exercise a non-P0087 path, per the DoD's own 'and the other two seeded symptoms' clause which currently has no assertion behind it.
findings.owner: T6 (own empty/null states) + INTEGRATION (validate a second seeded symptom)

findings.where: C2/T2 — scope/waveform is 'LATER' but observationMethod=waveform_capture is a live 9-enum value the show-rule must map TODAY
findings.detail: Scope/waveform is correctly scoped LATER (no waveform data in repo). BUT observation_method is a hard 9-value enum INCLUDING 'waveform_capture' (verified schema lines ~874-883), and a curator can author a test_action with that value right now. T3's resolveStepShape must map ALL 9 enum values; C2 ships NO scope stub; T4 has no waveform template. If a waveform_capture step appears in live data (the plan §6.2 explicitly says 'PWM likely carries electrical_measurement_at_pin NOT waveform_capture — confirm against live'), the chain has an unmapped shape -> runtime gap. The 'LATER' framing is fine for the part visual, but the SHAPE SELECTOR cannot have an unhandled enum arm.
findings.resolution: Require T3's resolveStepShape to map waveform_capture to an explicit honest-degrade shape (e.g. fall to single-value/PID-style or a 'no reference trace captured' neutral card per plan row), with a test asserting all 9 enum values are mapped (no unreachable arm). C2/T4 ship no scope visual but the selector must never throw/return undefined on the live enum value. Add 'all 9 observationMethod values mapped, waveform degrades, asserted by test' to T3 DoD (it says this in prose but make the all-9 test explicit).
findings.owner: T3 (map all 9 enum arms incl. waveform degrade + an exhaustiveness test)

findings.where: app/globals.css token block — T2 defines --role-*/--vt-recede/--vt-amber-600; T1 also claims to own globals.css token additions; T5 references; INTEGRATION blocks on them
findings.detail: Three units reference globals.css token ownership inconsistently. T2's outputs/files: 'MODIFY app/globals.css — add the six --role-* tokens, --vt-recede, --vt-amber-600.' But T6's dataOrAssetNeeds and INTEGRATION say these tokens are 'owned by T1/data-model' (T6: 'T1/data-model owns defining them'; INTEGRATION: 'owned by T2/T4'). C2 says 'T2/globals.css must define them.' So the SAME shared-file edit (the one cross-cutting CSS change everything visual blocks on) is attributed to T1 in some cards and T2 in others. If both edit the :root block, or neither does because each assumes the other, every visual track renders colorless and the leak/provenance colors are undefined. This is a real collision + a real orphan risk on the single most-depended-on shared file.
findings.resolution: Assign globals.css token definitions to exactly ONE owner and state it in every dependent card. Recommend T2 (it owns the visual vocabulary and the prototype's authoritative token values at proto-meter.html:18-42), landing them FIRST in the contract phase as an additive :root block. Strike the 'T1 owns the tokens' language from T1/T6/INTEGRATION and replace with 'consumes T2-defined tokens.' Make 'tokens defined in globals.css' a contract-phase gate (alongside C1/C2/C3) so no visual track forks before they exist.
findings.owner: T2 (sole owner of globals.css token additions, landed in contract phase) — T1/T5/T6/INTEGRATION consume only

findings.where: C1/T3/T4 — continuity/voltage-drop/duty-PWM sub-shapes require meterMode, but the selector input wiring is under-specified
findings.detail: observationMethod is a 9-enum but the spec needs 10 shapes: continuity-ground, voltage-drop, and duty-PWM all share observationMethod='electrical_measurement_at_pin' and are disambiguated ONLY by meterMode (plan §: 'Electrical sub-type (volts/ohms/drop/duty/amps) reads from test_actions.meterMode'). meterMode EXISTS (schema line ~886) but the loader DROPS it. C3's own risk flags this. The gap: selectStepShape's signature must take BOTH observationMethod AND meterMode (and stepKind), and meterMode is free-text today with NO agreed vocabulary (plan §8 item 6 open). If meterMode strings are unconstrained, continuity vs voltage-drop vs duty cannot be deterministically distinguished -> three v1 shapes are unreachable or mis-routed.
findings.resolution: Pin a minimal documented meterMode union in C1 NOW (e.g. 'volts'|'ohms'|'drop'|'duty'|'amps'|'pid'|'pressure'), nullable, so selectStepShape(observationMethod, meterMode, stepKind) can deterministically reach continuity-ground/voltage-drop/duty-PWM. Make C1 own the union (it owns the type shape); T1 surfaces the column as-is but C1 documents the expected values; T3's selector keys off it. Add a selector test covering electrical_measurement_at_pin x each meterMode -> distinct shape. Without the pinned union these three shapes are contractually unreachable.
findings.owner: C1 (pin the meterMode union, nullable) + T3 (3-arg selector + per-meterMode test)

findings.where: C2 (Part component API) vs T2 (Figma part kit) — both set ownsContract:"C2"; C2 writes components/diagnostic-diagram/parts/part-contract.ts, T2 writes components/diagram-kit/part-api.ts
findings.detail: TWO units declare themselves the OWNER of the C2 contract, in TWO different directories, with TWO different interface names (DiagramPartProps vs the part-api.ts prop interface) and TWO different registries (parts/part-registry.ts vs diagram-kit/registry.ts). Verified: neither components/diagnostic-diagram/ nor components/diagram-kit/ exists yet, so this is not a filesystem clash today — it is a definitional clash. If both run in parallel, C3/T3/T4/T6 (all of which import 'the C2 part-API') will import from one of two incompatible modules, and the stubs (C2) will not satisfy the real parts (T2). A contract cannot have two owners.
findings.resolution: Split the C2 ownership explicitly: ONE unit owns the TYPE (the prop interface + unions + registry signature), the other implements against it. Recommend C2-the-contract-unit owns components/diagnostic-diagram/parts/part-contract.ts (DiagramPartProps + PartKind/WireRole/PartTier/PartProvenance/Terminal/PartReading + the registry TYPE) and ships stubs; T2 STOPS declaring ownsContract:C2, deletes its components/diagram-kit/part-api.ts plan, and instead IMPLEMENTS part-contract.ts inside components/diagnostic-diagram/parts/ (or a sibling), populating the registry the contract declares. Pick ONE directory (components/diagnostic-diagram/parts/) and reserve it exclusively. T2's real-art components replace C2's stubs file-for-file, same interface.
findings.owner: C2 (Part component API) owns the contract+stubs; T2 (figma-kit) is downgraded to consumesContracts:[C1,C2] and implements parts into the C2-owned directory

findings.where: C3 (Slot/template interface) vs T3 (Assembly engine) — both set ownsContract:"C3"; C3 writes lib/diagnostics/diagram/slot-interface.ts, T3 writes slot-resolver.ts declaring SlotName/SlotAssignment/ResolvedScene
findings.detail: SlotName, ResolvedScene, SlotFill, AssembleScene and StepShape are declared as OUTPUTS of BOTH the C3 unit (slot-interface.ts) and the T3 unit (slot-resolver.ts: 'SlotName union + SlotAssignment type (C3 owns this)' and 'OWNS C3 slot-interface types'). T4 then 're-exports or imports the C3 slot-interface types T3 owns' — so T4 itself is unsure who owns them. Two parallel agents will each author the slot vocabulary + StepShape union; T4/T5/T6 will import a moving/duplicated target. This is the single most-imported type surface in the build, so divergence ripples into 4 units.
findings.resolution: Make C3 a real type-only contract unit that EXCLUSIVELY owns lib/diagnostics/diagram/slot-interface.ts (SlotName, StepShape, SlotFill, ResolvedScene, AssembleScene, StepTemplate, selectStepShape SIGNATURE, the per-shape required/optional/forbidden const table). T3 STOPS declaring ownsContract:C3 — it consumesContracts:[C1,C2,C3] and only IMPLEMENTS the bodies (selectStepShape, computeShowBudget, resolveSlots) importing the types from slot-interface.ts. show-rule.ts/slot-resolver.ts must NOT re-declare SlotName/ResolvedScene. T4 imports types from slot-interface.ts (never re-defines).
findings.owner: C3 (Slot/template interface) owns the type module; T3 (assembly-engine) is downgraded to consumer/implementer of C3

findings.where: T1 + C1 DoD: 'surface symptom_test_implications.priority on implicated test actions' vs loader reality (load-system-topology.ts:334-346, 456)
findings.detail: Verified the loader surfaces implication as a BOOLEAN only: implRows selects just testActionId, builds a Set, and TopologyTestAction.implicatedByCurrentSymptom = implicatedIds.has(t.id) (line 456). There is NO priority anywhere in the loader; priority lives only in lib/diagnostics/cached-lookup.ts:164/183/223. Both C1 and T1 list 'surface priority' as an output/DoD, but T1's filesAndAreas/outputs describe the migration+SELECT widening and do NOT actually scope the implRows->priority change or the Set->Map refactor. Step ORDERING (a step = a test_action ordered by priority) is load-bearing for T3/T6 ('order = symptom_test_implications.priority'), so if priority is not surfaced, the whole step sequence is unordered. The current Set cannot carry priority.
findings.resolution: Add an explicit T1 task: change implRows SELECT to also select symptomTestImplications.priority, replace the implicatedIds Set with a Map<testActionId,priority>, add priority:number|null to TopologyTestAction, and default null for non-implicated actions. C1 freezes the field name/type. Add a loadSystemTopology round-trip assertion that an implicated action carries its priority and a non-implicated one is null. Without this, T3's 'receives the already-selected current step' input has no producer.
findings.owner: T1 (data deltas) implements; C1 (data contract) freezes the priority field shape

findings.where: T1 isOutOfRange map shape vs topology-detail-panel.tsx:234 (KEPT consumer) — pinScenarioReadings PK is (pin_id, scenario_id)
findings.detail: Verified pinScenarioReadings is keyed (pinId,scenarioId) and the loader assembles a flat Record<string,string> pinReadings keyed by pinId per scenario (load-system-topology.ts:404-407). The KEPT detail-panel reads scenario.pinReadings[pin.id] directly (topology-detail-panel.tsx:234). If T1 surfaces isOutOfRange by ENRICHING pinReadings into an object ({reading,isOutOfRange}) it breaks that string-index access and every other reader of pinReadings — a non-additive change that violates the additive-loader constraint. T1's own risk note flags this but leaves the choice open ('confirm with T3'), which means two units could assume different shapes.
findings.resolution: LOCK the additive shape now in C1: add a SIBLING map TopologyScenario.isOutOfRange: Record<string,boolean> (pinId->bool), leaving pinReadings: Record<string,string> untouched. Missing key => not-out-of-range => neutral. Add it to the readingRows SELECT (isOutOfRange) and a parallel fold in the scenario assembly loop (lines 405-407). Assert in the loader test that pinReadings stays a string map. This makes detail-panel and scenario-bar compile unchanged.
findings.owner: C1 (data contract) locks the sibling-map shape; T1 (data deltas) implements in load-system-topology.ts and schema.ts

findings.where: T6 'topology-selection-context.tsx — DELETE or fold into assembler' vs the KEPT topology-detail-panel.tsx which consumes the selection
findings.detail: Verified topology-selection-context.tsx is imported by FIVE files: topology-diagram.tsx, topology-flow.ts, topology-node.tsx (all in the DELETE set) AND topology-detail-panel.tsx (which T6 lists as KEEP: 'scenario chip, footer, formatters are kept surfaces'). T6 says the detail-panel is 'driven by component/pin/connection selection from the xyflow node tree' and flags it as an open risk, but its DoD keeps the detail-panel mounting. You cannot DELETE topology-selection-context.tsx while keeping a consumer that needs a selection source. In the assembler world there are no node-clicks, so the selection contract is undefined. This is a C3 contract gap: how does a step's focus/selected part get expressed to the detail panel?
findings.resolution: Decide the selection model in C3 BEFORE T6/T3 build: either (a) the detail-panel is folded into the per-step Meter/reading surface (drop topology-selection-context entirely, and C3.ResolvedScene exposes the device-under-test + selected part), or (b) keep a slimmed selection provider that the assembler feeds. Add a C3 field (e.g. ResolvedScene.focus / selectedPartId) so the panel has a data source that is NOT node-clicks. Sequence: this decision is a contract item, not a T6 integration detail.
findings.owner: C3 (slot-interface) defines how focus/selection is expressed; T6 wires it (consumer)

findings.where: selectStepShape coverage: observationMethod is a 9-enum (verified schema.ts:873-885) but the v1 catalog needs 10 StepShapes; meterMode is DROPPED by the loader and stepKind does not exist
findings.detail: Verified the 9 observationMethod values include exactly ONE electrical method (electrical_measurement_at_pin) and waveform_capture. The 10 v1 shapes (confirm/electrical-probe/continuity-ground/single-pid/pressure-flow/look-inspect/locate/fork/duty-pwm/voltage-drop) cannot be derived from observationMethod alone: electrical-probe vs continuity-ground vs voltage-drop vs duty-pwm all share electrical_measurement_at_pin and split on meterMode; locate needs stepKind; fork needs branch presence. meterMode EXISTS (schema:886) but the loader SELECT (296-306) DROPS it, and stepKind is proposed-new. If selectStepShape's signature takes only observationMethod, four shapes are unreachable.
findings.resolution: Pin the selector signature in C3 to selectStepShape(action: {observationMethod; meterMode|null; stepKind|null; hasBranches:boolean}) -> StepShape, and make C1/T1's DoD a HARD prerequisite that meterMode is surfaced (it is the cheapest un-drop: add one line to testActionRows SELECT) and stepKind exists nullable. Until both land, T3 must degrade electrical to plain electrical-probe and treat absent stepKind as 'not locate'. Add a coverage test asserting every observationMethod x meterMode combo maps to exactly one shape.
findings.owner: C1/T1 surface meterMode+stepKind; C3 freezes the selectStepShape signature; T3 implements

findings.where: app/globals.css — six --role-* + --vt-recede + --vt-amber-600 tokens (verified UNDEFINED in globals.css but referenced by components/topology/topology.css:283-288 etc.) edited by T2, while T5 may add 375px overrides
findings.detail: Verified: globals.css defines none of --role-signal/--role-5v-ref/--role-low-ref/--role-pwm/--role-12v/--role-ground/--vt-recede/--vt-amber-600, yet topology.css references all of them (lines 136,199,251,283-288,412+). So the LIVE topology already renders these wires/colors as undefined (transparent/black) today — this is a pre-existing latent bug, not just a new dependency. T2 owns adding them; T5, T4, T3-tests, T6 and INTEGRATION ALL block on them. globals.css is a shared mutable file (T2 adds tokens; T5 'must NOT' but might add mobile overrides).
findings.resolution: Make 'define the 8 tokens in globals.css with the proto-meter.html values (lines 18-42)' a CONTRACT-PHASE deliverable that lands with C1/C2 BEFORE any track forks, not buried inside T2's later figma work. Reserve the globals.css :root vt-token block for T2 only (additive, no edits to existing vt-bone/vt-signal). T5 references/overrides via its own components/diagram/diagram-mobile.css, never edits globals.css. Sequence: tokens land in Wave 1.
findings.owner: T2 (figma-kit) defines tokens early as a contract-phase deliverable; T5 only consumes

findings.where: T4 templates vs T5 mobile — both edit the per-step-shape template files / their CSS (T4 owns step-template + template CSS, T5 adds @media blocks to them)
findings.detail: T5's own analysis names this 'the sharpest coordination risk': T5 must ADD responsive hooks/@media blocks to files T4 OWNS (step-template.tsx and its CSS), and the two scope cards even disagree on the directory (T4 -> lib/diagnostics/diagram/templates/; T5 -> components/diagram/). Concurrent edits to the same TSX/CSS files are a direct merge collision, and there is no agreed coordinate-space convention (prototype uses fixed 1320x760 world; new system may want normalized coords) — without that, desktop templates (T4) and mobile variants (T5) disagree on geometry.
findings.resolution: Two upfront agreements before T4/T5 fork: (1) T4 freezes a stable root class + data-attribute per step-shape and a documented coordinate convention (recommend normalized 0..1 slot coords + a framing HINT carried by the template, applied by the renderer); T5 then writes ONLY CSS targeting those classes in its own diagram-mobile.css and never edits T4's TSX. (2) Resolve the directory split: templates are layout-logic (lib/diagnostics/diagram/templates/) but the rendering React lives in components/diagram/ — assign each file to exactly one owner. Make this a written T4->T5 seam, sequenced T4-first.
findings.owner: T4 (templates) freezes the class/coordinate contract first; T5 (mobile) consumes via CSS only

findings.where: C1/C2/C3 'contracts land first' — the decomposition asserts T2-T6 'fork once contracts exist' but T3 owns C3 and T2 owns C2, creating a circular fork order
findings.detail: The stated order 'contracts land first, then T1-T6 fork' is internally inconsistent given the ownership as written: C3 cannot 'land first' if T3 owns/authors it, and C2 cannot 'land first' if T2 owns/authors it — the contracts would land only WHEN their owning tracks run, defeating the foundation-first ordering. Combined with the two ownership-collision blockers above, there is no clean wave boundary. The dependency edges as written are: C1 depends on T1 (for the two new columns), but T1 also dependsOn nothing and 'owns C1' — so C1 and T1 are the same work split across two cards.
findings.resolution: Collapse to explicit waves with single owners. WAVE 0 (type-only, no parallelism, must merge first): C1 type shape + loader-SELECT signatures (folded with T1's schema.ts col defs since T1 'owns C1'), C2 part-contract.ts, C3 slot-interface.ts, AND the 8 globals.css tokens. WAVE 1 (parallel, fork only after Wave 0 merges): T1 migration+SELECT bodies, T2 part implementations+Figma, T3 selectStepShape/resolveSlots bodies, T4 template modules, T5 mobile CSS. WAVE 2 (serial, single owner, last): T6 app-swap. WAVE 3: INTEGRATION. Make every contract unit type-only with ZERO runtime so it can truly land before its track.
findings.owner: new unit: 'Wave 0 — contract freeze' (a single coordinated merge of C1-types/C2-contract/C3-types/tokens) before any track forks

findings.where: T1 vs C1 both edit lib/db/schema.ts (testActions + pinScenarioReadings table blocks) and lib/diagnostics/load-system-topology.ts type exports
findings.detail: C1's filesAndAreas lists MODIFY on schema.ts (add stepKind/isOutOfRange) AND load-system-topology.ts type exports + SELECTs; T1's filesAndAreas lists the SAME edits to the SAME line regions (schema testActions ~886, pinScenarioReadings 1099-1113; loader 297/318/386 + types 30/36/69). Both also touch drizzle/migrations/meta/_journal.json (verified at idx 25, next is 26). These are not two parallel units — they are one edit surface described twice. Run in parallel they guarantee a merge conflict on schema.ts and the loader.
findings.resolution: Treat C1 and T1 as ONE unit (or strictly: C1 = the type-only freeze in Wave 0, T1 = the migration DDL + SELECT body in Wave 1, with C1 landing/merging fully before T1 starts). No concurrent edits to schema.ts/load-system-topology.ts/_journal.json by two agents. T1 appends journal idx 26 (breakpoints:true) and writes 0024 with --> statement-breakpoint between the two ALTERs (verified repo hazard: missing breakpoints silently breaks PGlite).
findings.owner: C1 (data contract) = Wave 0 type freeze; T1 (data deltas) = Wave 1 implementer; never concurrent

findings.where: INTEGRATION leak-test determinism vs C3 not exposing the per-step resolved element set
findings.detail: INTEGRATION's DoD requires a deterministic test (tests/unit/diagnostic-scene-assembly.test.ts) asserting 'a pressure step shows zero electrical terminals in the rendered set'. But that is only machine-checkable if C3.ResolvedScene exposes the resolved part/terminal set per step (computeShowBudget.pinsAllowed + resolveSlots output). INTEGRATION's own risk note flags this ('if C3 does not expose the resolved element set per step, integration can only validate visually'). The leak-class bug is the headline goal; if the contract doesn't make it statically checkable, the guard degrades to screenshot-eyeballing.
findings.resolution: Add to C3 an explicit ResolvedScene field that enumerates the rendered element set (e.g. parts[], wires[], terminals[], overlay|null) and a computeShowBudget.pinsAllowed boolean, so the integration test can assert terminals.length===0 on pressure/PID/look/locate shapes. Bake the forbidden-slot table (already in C3 outputs) as the source of truth the test imports. This converts the leak guard from visual to deterministic.
findings.owner: C3 (slot-interface) exposes the resolved element set + pinsAllowed; INTEGRATION asserts against it

findings.where: INTEGRATION + T6 both edit components/screens/topology-diagnostic.tsx and app/curator/topology/page.tsx (the ONLY route)
findings.detail: Both T6 and INTEGRATION list MODIFY/wire on topology-diagnostic.tsx and page.tsx. INTEGRATION correctly states it 'MUST run LAST' and 'is NOT parallel-safe with T6', but both still claim edit rights on the same two files. Verified these are the only two files consuming loadSystemTopology/layoutTopology/TopologyDiagnostic. Low risk only because the sequencing (T6 then INTEGRATION) is already acknowledged, but the edit-ownership is ambiguous.
findings.resolution: Assign topology-diagnostic.tsx and page.tsx edits EXCLUSIVELY to T6. INTEGRATION gets only NEW files (.design-shots/scene-walk.mjs, tests/unit/diagnostic-scene-assembly.test.ts) and READ-ONLY access to the screen/route; any change INTEGRATION needs is reported back as a T6 gap. Sequence T6 -> INTEGRATION strictly serial (no overlap).
findings.owner: T6 (app-swap) owns the screen+route edits; INTEGRATION is read-only + new-files-only, runs last

findings.where: fork StepShape 'route' slot depends on branch_logic.routesToTestActionId (DROPPED by loader, verified branchRows SELECT 318-323 selects only condition/verdict/nextAction) — nextAction free-text already exists (schema:925)
findings.detail: Verified branchLogic has BOTH nextAction (text, notNull, schema:925) AND routesToTestActionId (uuid FK, schema:926-930), but the loader SELECT surfaces only nextAction; routesToTestActionId+reasoning are dropped. C3/T3/T4 all want the fork's 'route' slot to point at the next test_action via routesToTestActionId. If T1 doesn't surface it, fork degrades to a text-only nextAction route. The risk is that SlotFill is typed assuming the id exists, then breaks when it's null.
findings.resolution: C1/T1 add routesToTestActionId+reasoning to branchRows SELECT and TopologyBranch (additive). C3's SlotFill route arm must permit a degraded text-only route (nextAction string) when routesToTestActionId is null — bake the honest-thin-data arm into the type now so fork is buildable against today's data (which has routesToTestActionId likely null) without a second contract change.
findings.owner: C1/T1 surface routesToTestActionId; C3 types the degraded text-only route arm

findings.where: INTEGRATION + T6 'render the real 6.7L scene' validation vs scene-data.json being parts-only (verified 8 top keys, NO test_actions/branch_logic)
findings.detail: Verified .design-shots/scene-data.json keys are exactly [platform,symptom,componentCount,components,pins,connections,scenarios,pinReadings] — no test_actions, no branch_logic, no scenario_wire_states. So the step/fork/expected-* layer (the whole point of the assembler) CANNOT be exercised from the fixture; it requires live Supabase via the real loader. Multiple units (C1, T3, T4, INTEGRATION) lean on scene-data.json as their validation fixture, but it only proves component/pin/connection field-presence, not the step pipeline. Risk: a track 'validates' against the fixture and believes it's done while the step path is untested.
findings.resolution: State in C1/T3/T4/INTEGRATION that scene-data.json validates ONLY part/wire rendering; the step/show-rule/fork/expected pipeline MUST validate against live Supabase (ford-super-duty-4th-gen-67-psd / p0087) through loadSystemTopology. INTEGRATION's scene-walk.mjs already targets the live route — make that the authoritative step-pipeline gate. Add a unit-test fixture that includes synthetic test_actions/branches (not scene-data.json) for the deterministic leak test, since the JSON lacks them.
findings.owner: INTEGRATION owns the live-route step gate; T3/T4 use a synthetic test_action fixture, not scene-data.json, for step-shape tests

lenses.summary: The decomposition's biggest danger is NOT cross-track collision once forked — it is that the contract layer itself has two unresolved ownership collisions and a circular fork order, so 'contracts land first' cannot actually happen as written. THREE BLOCKERS: (1) C2 is owned by both the C2 unit and T2 in two different directories with two different interface names — a contract cannot have two owners; (2) C3 (SlotName/StepShape/ResolvedScene — the most-imported type surface) is owned by both the C3 unit and T3 in different files; (3) the fork order is circular because the contract-owning tracks (T2 owns C2, T3 owns C3) would only land their contracts WHEN they run, defeating foundation-first. RESOLUTION: collapse to explicit waves. WAVE 0 (single coordinated merge, no parallelism): make all three contracts TYPE-ONLY with single owners — C1 type shape + loader signatures (folded with T1's schema col defs since they edit the identical schema.ts/loader regions), C2 part-contract.ts (T2 downgraded to implementer), C3 slot-interface.ts (T3 downgraded to implementer), PLUS the 8 globals.css tokens (verified undefined today, already silently breaking live topology.css). WAVE 1 forks cleanly: T1 migration+SELECT bodies, T2 part art, T3 selectStepShape/resolveSlots bodies, T4 templates, T5 mobile CSS. WAVE 2: T6 app-swap (serial, owns the screen+route exclusively). WAVE 3: INTEGRATION (read-only, new-files-only). Verified facts that force several contract fixes: priority is NOT surfaced by the loader (it uses a boolean Set, ordering is impossible without a T1 Set->Map change); isOutOfRange must be a sibling pinId->bool map or it breaks topology-detail-panel.tsx:234's string-index read; selectStepShape needs meterMode (dropped) + stepKind (new) or 4 of 10 shapes are unreachable from the 9-enum; topology-selection-context.tsx is consumed by the KEPT detail-panel so it can't be deleted without C3 defining a non-node-click focus model; and the leak guard is only deterministic if C3 exposes the per-step resolved element set. With the wave boundaries + single-owner contracts in place, T1-T5 are genuinely parallel-safe (each owns disjoint new files); the only real concurrent-edit hazard left is T4<->T5 on template files, fixed by T4 freezing a stable root-class + coordinate convention so T5 writes CSS-only.

changesFromOriginal: COLLAPSED the three duplicate contract cards into their owning tracks. C1 was authored by both a standalone 'C1 Data contract' card AND T1 (identical edits to schema.ts + load-system-topology.ts + promote-system-data.test.ts) — merged into T1. C2 was owned by both a standalone card (components/diagnostic-diagram/parts/) AND T2 (components/diagram-kit/) — merged into T2 at one path. C3 was owned by both a standalone card AND T3 (both declaring SlotName/StepShape/ResolvedScene) — merged into T3.

Re-cast the three contracts as a single Wave 0 'contract freeze' that is TYPE-ONLY and lands/merges before any track forks. C1 = type shape + loader-SELECT signatures + schema col defs (no migration body, no SELECT body). C2 = part-contract.ts + unions + registry signature + stubs. contracts.name: C1 — data-contract (additive loader + type shape)
contracts.owner: T1 (data) — sole owner; lands as Wave 0 type-only freeze, implements bodies in Wave 1
contracts.definition: Extends loadSystemTopology -> SystemTopology ADDITIVELY ONLY. New optional keys, no existing key renamed/removed. EXACT shape: (1) TopologyTestAction gains meterMode: MeterMode|null, expectedValue: number|null, expectedUnit: string|null, expectedTolerance: number|null, stepKind: string|null, priority: number|null (the symptom_test_implications.priority for this implicated action; null when not implicated). Existing observationMethod/implicatedByCurrentSymptom/branches/expectedObservation/sourceProvenance stay. (2) TopologyBranch gains routesToTestActionId: string|null, reasoning: string|null (in addition to existing condition/verdict/nextAction — verdict is ALREADY surfaced, do not re-add). (3) TopologyScenario gains a SIBLING map isOutOfRange: Record<string, boolean> (pinId -> out-of-range; missing key => not-out-of-range => neutral). pinReadings stays Record<string,string> UNCHANGED (topology-detail-panel.tsx reads it as a string index). (4) MeterMode = 'volts'|'ohms'|'drop'|'duty'|'amps'|'pid'|'pressure' (nullable, documented union) so the shape selector can split the single electrical observationMethod into probe/continuity/voltage-drop/duty. (5) Verdict-precedence note: isOutOfRange authoritative -> branch verdict==='fail' -> neutral graphite default; NO prose-number parsing; numeric compare deferred (no tech_outcomes loaded). FACTS LOCKED: electricalRole/fromPinId/toPinId/connectionKind are ALREADY surfaced (loader 236-243) — DO NOT touch the connection SELECT. branch verdict ALREADY surfaced (321). The ONLY genuine un-drops are the 4 meter fields + routesToTestActionId/reasoning + surfacing priority (change implRows from a Set to a Map). The ONLY genuinely-new columns are test_actions.step_kind and pin_scenario_readings.is_out_of_range.

contracts.name: C2 — part-API (DiagramPartProps)
contracts.owner: T2 (figma-kit) — sole owner; contract + typed stubs land Wave 0, real art Wave 1
contracts.definition: The single TS prop interface every kit part implements, in components/diagram-kit/part-api.ts. DiagramPartProps + supporting unions: PartKind (the 8 components.kind values pump|sensor|actuator|valve|module|mechanical|splice|connector), PartRoleSpecial (ground|relay|fuse|power-source), WireRole (the 6 electricalRole values), PartTier ('focus'|'anchor'|'recede'), PartProvenance ('drafted'|'field-verified'|'needs-field-check'), Terminal {id, role, edge, label, visible, active, selected} where visible is engine-controlled (the leak-lock: terminals never always-on), PartReading (thin handoff to the existing Meter — C2 does NOT re-own the gauge). All meter/step fields (observationMethod, stepKind) live on the STEP not the part — parts render what the engine tells them. Plus a part-registry mapping (kind|roleSpecial) -> component so consumers resolve by data not a switch. REDUCED-MOTION is a contract requirement: any animated part (wire flow, pulse) gates its animation on prefers-reduced-motion at the part level. stepKind/isOutOfRange carried OPTIONAL so C2 typechecks whether or not the migration has landed. NO scope/waveform stub in v1. Single directory root reserved: components/diagram-kit/.

contracts.name: C3 — slot-interface (the data<->layout seam)
contracts.owner: T3 (assembly-engine) — sole owner; type-only module lands Wave 0, bodies Wave 1
contracts.definition: Type-only module lib/diagnostics/diagram/slot-interface.ts — the single declaration site for: (1) closed SlotName union: 'source'|'device-under-test'|'ground'|'downstream-anchor'|'overlay'|'gauge'|'good-vs-bad'|'route'|'location'|'detail'|'quiet-field' (the 'detail' arm carries the why/see-source/Operational-Theori prose payload; 'quiet-field' is the single confirm-shape whole-spine backdrop, NOT a reuse of device-under-test). (2) StepShape union covering all 10 v1 shapes: confirm|electrical-probe|continuity-ground|single-pid|pressure-flow|look-inspect|locate|fork|duty-pwm|voltage-drop. (3) selectStepShape SIGNATURE (body in T3): (observationMethod, meterMode|null, stepKind|null, hasBranches) -> StepShape — keys ONLY on these inputs; templates/registry must NOT re-derive from observationMethod. (4) SlotFill (one of: C2 part-ref + props | wire-set | the single overlay primitive | gauge/Meter payload | a content payload for 'detail' | a degraded text-only route arm | null). (5) ResolvedScene = { shape, slots: Record<SlotName, SlotFill>, activeWireIds, overlay|null, gaugeSpec|null, forkRoute|null, focus: {selectedPartId}, pinsAllowed: boolean, elements: ResolvedElement[] } — elements is the FLAT enumerable rendered set (parts+wires+terminals+overlay) so the leak test asserts deterministically. (6) AssembleScene = (topology, step, activeScenario) => ResolvedScene and StepTemplate = (scene) => ReactNode signatures. (7) a typed per-shape const table of required/optional/FORBIDDEN slots encoding the hard rule: terminals/overlay ONLY on electrical shapes; pressure-flow forbids electrical slots; locate suppresses gauge; fork = one route slot. SELECTION CONTRACT: v1 has NO free node-click selection — the step IS the selection; detail comes from the 'detail' slot. No 'AI' string, no 'step N of M' anywhere.
